novel_features.name: Reuse-last with budget gate
novel_features.description: Recompose your previous order locally, validate against tax, and refuse to fire if total exceeds your --max cap.
novel_features.rationale: Requires composing the cart from local order history, hitting /m/api/orders to add tax, and gating against a user-provided cap before any payment surface opens.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent needs to know 'will this fit my budget' before committing — answer comes from one structured call with no browser involvement.

novel_features.name: Headless full-auto place
novel_features.description: Drive a headless Chrome via chromedp to complete the Braintree DropIn flow with your saved card, with the budget cap enforced before the browser opens.
novel_features.rationale: OrderToGo's payment uses Braintree's client-side single-use nonce; nothing else can submit a real order without re-implementing Braintree's client SDK or driving a real browser.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent has a budget-validated plan and the user has confirmed; this is the one command that actually moves money.

novel_features.name: What's my usual
novel_features.description: Cluster your historical orders by item-set similarity and surface the recurring set that defines 'your usual' at any restaurant you order from.
novel_features.rationale: Requires local sync of all orders and similarity grouping over the items array — the website only shows discrete past orders, not patterns.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a user says 'order my usual' and the agent needs to decide whether one obvious pattern exists or whether to ask which usual.

novel_features.name: Spending analytics
novel_features.description: Total spent, average order, days since last order, top items, and weekly cadence — all from local order history with one SQL query.
novel_features.rationale: The website shows a list of orders but no totals, no cadence, no top-items. Local sync + SQL turns the same data into habits.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to answer 'how much have I spent here' or 'how often do I order' without re-fetching history.

novel_features.name: Auto-tip from history
novel_features.description: Pass --tip auto and the CLI applies your average tip percentage from history at this restaurant.
novel_features.rationale: Requires reading historical tips for the restaurant from local store and computing the mean; the website always defaults to $0.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent doesn't want to make the user pick a tip percentage — go with what's habitual.

novel_features.name: Closed-check before place
novel_features.description: Refuse to fire if the restaurant is not currently open at the requested pickup time, using cached hours.
novel_features.rationale: The web only surfaces 'closed' at submit time after a network round trip; we check locally before any cart pre-validate.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Catches a class of agent failures before the payment flow opens.

novel_features.name: Agent-context snapshot
novel_features.description: Single-call structured dump: account, default restaurant, your usual, last-order summary, budget hint, days-since-last.
novel_features.rationale: Designed for an agent that needs to answer 'order my usual' in one call — gathers state from multiple endpoints and the local store and returns one JSON object.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent enters a session and needs a complete picture before suggesting any action.

novel_features.name: Verify-env safe by construction
novel_features.description: All side-effect commands short-circuit when PRINTING_PRESS_VERIFY=1 is set, printing 'would place: <plan>' instead of submitting.
novel_features.rationale: Required by Printing Press conventions; ensures dogfood / CI / agent-test runs cannot accidentally trigger a real order.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent or test harness wants to exercise the place path without real-world consequences.

narrative.headline: Browse, cart, and place orders at any OrderToGo.com restaurant from the terminal — pure-Go agent-native client with a hard budget gate and headless Braintree drive.
narrative.value_prop: OrderToGo.com is a multi-tenant pickup ordering platform that powers small chains across multiple US metros. There is no CLI, MCP, or SDK presence anywhere for it. This CLI gives an agent every read endpoint, a safe `order plan` that composes a cart locally and validates against a `--max` cap, and an `order place` that drives a headless Chrome through Braintree DropIn to actually submit. Everything is offline-cacheable, agent-callable, and verify-env safe by construction.
narrative.auth_narrative: OrderToGo authenticates via Firebase phone-OTP on the web. The CLI does not implement phone OTP — instead, `auth login --chrome` imports your existing OrderToGo session cookies from your local Chrome profile (default user-data-dir, default profile). After import, every command travels with the cookie; refresh by re-running `auth login --chrome` if your session expires.

quickstart.comment: Import OrderToGo session cookies from Chrome - no password, no OTP from the CLI
Pull full order history once so 'usual' detection and analytics work offline
Optional - by default the CLI uses your most-frequent restaurant from the last 30 days
Confirm the most recent order - --reuse-last will recompose this exact cart
Recompose, hit /m/api/orders for tax, assert total <= cap before any payment surface opens
Drive headless Chrome through Braintree with your saved card; only fires after the budget gate has already passed

troubleshoots.fix: Pass --chrome-profile 'Profile 1' or --user-data-dir <path> if you use a non-default Chrome profile

troubleshoots.symptom: order place fails inside Braintree DropIn (saved card not selected)
troubleshoots.fix: Re-run with --visible to drop into a visible Chrome window and tap Place Order yourself; the CLI still confirms via order-history polling
Run `restaurants show <slug> --closed-check` first; the CLI refuses to plan against a closed restaurant after a sync of hours
Either raise --max or pass --confirm-over-budget; the CLI never silently exceeds the cap
Run `sync --menu <slug>` to refresh the menu cache before composing

troubleshoots.symptom: No restaurants show up in `restaurants list`
troubleshoots.fix: Pass --location <code> (e.g. sto for Seattle area, det for Detroit) — the CLI doesn't assume a metro and reads location codes from your synced order history

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when an agent needs to handle 'order my usual', 'is the restaurant open', 'how much have I spent here this month', or 'place this exact cart with a budget cap'. It's read-fast (everything cached locally after one sync), write-safe (every place command is gated behind --confirm + --max + verify-env short-circuit), and works for any OrderToGo customer regardless of metro.

recipes.title: Order your usual under a cap
recipes.explanation: Compose your usual cart, validate with tax, and emit only the bottom-line numbers + item names. The /m/api/orders response includes every cart field; --select keeps the agent context tight.

recipes.title: Place after agent confirmation (verify-env safe)
recipes.explanation: Run only after the agent has shown the plan to the user and the user has approved. Under PRINTING_PRESS_VERIFY=1 the CLI prints 'would place' without launching Chrome - useful for dry-run agent flows.

recipes.title: Single-call agent dump
recipes.explanation: Returns account, default restaurant, last-order summary, your usual, budget hint, days-since-last - everything an agent needs to decide whether to suggest 'order my usual'.

recipes.title: Find a restaurant in your area
recipes.explanation: List all restaurants in the Seattle (sto) location. Replace sto with your metro code (det, sea, etc) - reads from synced order history when available.

recipes.title: Show recent order history
recipes.explanation: Hit /m/api/getmicmeshorders directly for your latest orders. Combine with --select to keep payloads tight.

trigger_phrases: how much have I spent at
